key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed FEM with Wachspress interpolation enables nonlinear geotechnical analysis on flexible polygonal and hybrid quadtree meshes without direct polygonal shape-function derivatives.
engineering_relevance: The framework improves robustness for staged excavation, local failure, and mesh-sensitive geotechnical problems while preserving accurate prediction of displacement, stress, plastic strain, bearing capacity, and slope safety.
method: Uses cell-based strain smoothing with boundary integration over polygonal subcells.
Implements incremental elasto-plastic updates for Mohr-Coulomb and Duncan-Chang constitutive models.
Integrated into ABAQUS via a user element to support standard polygonal and hanging-node quadtree meshes.
evidence_signal: Verified on benchmark and engineering cases including a perforated plate, strip footing, core rockfill dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability, with reported accuracy and improved mesh flexibility/efficiency.
watch_item: Accuracy and robustness depend on reliable user-element implementation and constitutive calibration, especially for highly nonlinear plasticity and complex staged construction.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
